steps.action: Tarantella (Italian: [taranˈtɛlla]) is a group o with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Tarantella (Italian: [taranˈtɛlla]) is a group o with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It is among the most recognized forms of traditi
steps.text: It is among the most recognized forms of traditional southern Italian music.

steps.action: The specific dance-name varies with every region
steps.text: The specific dance-name varies with every region, for instance sonu a ballu in Calabria, tammurriata in Campania, and pizzica in Salento.

steps.action: This type of dance became known as the tarantell
steps.text: This type of dance became known as the tarantella.

steps.action: Lowe Thompson proposed that the dance is a survi
steps.text: Lowe Thompson proposed that the dance is a survival from a "Dianic or Dionysiac cult", driven underground.

steps.action: However, other forms of the dance were and still
steps.text: However, other forms of the dance were and still are dances of couples usually either mimicking courtship or a sword fight.

steps.action: The confusion appears to derive from the fact th
steps.text: The confusion appears to derive from the fact that the spiders, the condition, its sufferers (tarantolati), and the dances all have names similar to the city of Taranto.

steps.action: The dance originated in the Apulia region, and s
steps.text: The dance originated in the Apulia region, and spread throughout the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies.

steps.action: The Neapolitan tarantella is a courtship dance p
steps.text: The Neapolitan tarantella is a courtship dance performed by couples whose "rhythms, melodies, gestures, and accompanying songs are quite distinct" featuring faster more cheerful music.

steps.action: The "magico-religious" tarantella is a solo danc
steps.text: The "magico-religious" tarantella is a solo dance performed supposedly to cure through perspiration the delirium and contortions attributed to the bite of a spider at harvest (summer) time.

steps.action: The dance was later applied as a supposed cure f
steps.text: The dance was later applied as a supposed cure for the behavior of neurotic women (carnevaletto delle donne).

steps.action: There are several traditional tarantella groups:
steps.text: There are several traditional tarantella groups: Cantori di Carpino, Officina Zoé, Uccio Aloisi gruppu, Canzoniere Grecanico Salentino, Selva Cupina, and I Tamburellisti di Torrepaduli.

steps.action: The tarantella is a dance in which the dancer an
steps.text: The tarantella is a dance in which the dancer and the drum player constantly try to upstage each other by playing faster or dancing longer than the other, subsequently tiring one person out first.

steps.action: Reportedly, victims who had collapsed or were co
steps.text: Reportedly, victims who had collapsed or were convulsing would begin to dance with appropriate music and be revived as if a tarantula had bitten them.

steps.action: The music used to treat dancing mania appears to
steps.text: The music used to treat dancing mania appears to be similar to that used in the case of tarantism, although little is known about either.

steps.action: Entire communities of people would join hands, d
steps.text: Entire communities of people would join hands, dance, leap, scream, and shake for hours [.

steps.action: ] lively, shrill tunes, played on trumpets and f
steps.text: ] lively, shrill tunes, played on trumpets and fifes, excited the dancers; soft, calm harmonies, graduated from fast to slow, high to low, prove efficacious for the cure.

steps.action: Global Tarantella: Reinventing Southern Italian
steps.text: Global Tarantella: Reinventing Southern Italian Folk Music and Dances.

steps.text: The Encyclopedia of World Folk Dance.

steps.action: It is characterized by a fast upbeat tempo, usua
steps.text: It is characterized by a fast upbeat tempo, usually in 68 time (sometimes 128 or 44), accompanied by tambourines.

steps.action: Tarantella is popular in southern Italy, Greece,
steps.text: Tarantella is popular in southern Italy, Greece, and Malta.

steps.action: The term may appear as tarantello in a linguisti
steps.text: The term may appear as tarantello in a linguistically masculine construction.

steps.action: The place was a colony of ancient Greece, and ev
steps.text: The place was a colony of ancient Greece, and even Napoli comes from the Greek word Neapolis, which means 'New City'.

steps.action: Before the unification of Italy, it was ruled by
steps.text: Before the unification of Italy, it was ruled by Spain and briefly by Austria.
